plainEnglish: Content must reflow into a single column without requiring two-dimensional scrolling at a width of 320 CSS pixels (equivalent to 400% zoom on a 1280px screen). Users shouldn't have to scroll both horizontally and vertically to read. Use responsive, flexible layouts; only things like data tables or maps may scroll in two dimensions.
whyItMatters: Low-vision users who zoom to 400% are forced to scroll back and forth on every line when content doesn't reflow to a single column.
quickCheck: Set the viewport to 320px wide (Chrome DevTools device toolbar) or zoom to 400% and confirm no horizontal scrolling is needed to read the content.
commonMistakes: Fixed-width (px) containers that don't shrink below the viewport, causing horizontal scroll.
Multi-column layouts that don't collapse to a single column on narrow viewports.
A min-width on the page, or large fixed-position elements, that overflow at 320px.
Using white-space: nowrap on long content so it can't wrap.

codeExamples.label: Layout that reflows to one column
Fixed-width layout that forces horizontal scroll
